The Novato City Council comprises five elected officials who make policy decisions for the City of Novato. The City Council sets priorities, approves the budget, and addresses citywide issues affecting the Novato community. They are elected by district to overlapping, four-year terms. The Councilmembers annually select the Mayor and Mayor Pro-Tem (Vice Mayor).

Additionally, the Council appoints the City Manager, City Attorney, and City Commissions. The Mayor, also a member of the Council, signs official documents, chairs the Council meetings, and acts as the official head of the City at public and ceremonial occasions. The Council Policy Manual sets forth policies and procedures by which the City Council, Boards, Commissions, Standing and Ad Hoc Committees, City Manager, City Attorney and City Clerk conduct City Council business and activities. (Note: Appendices Sections 6.01 - 6.08 are not posted. To request, please contact the City Clerk.)

  • Agendas & Minutes
  • What's My District?
  • Elections
  • Strategic Plan
  • Report an Issue
Regular Meeting Schedule

The Novato City Council meets most 2nd & 4th Tuesday of the month at Novato City Hall, 901 Sherman Avenue, with some exceptions.

Always check the Agenda or the Calendar to determine the correct meeting location and time. Live captioning and assistive listening options are provided during meetings.

Public Comments

Provide online public comments to the City Council using  eComment. The eComment period opens when the City Council agenda is posted online and closes 3 hours before the meeting begins.

Comments can also be given during the meeting in-person. Public participation options will be available on the agenda, which will be posted at least 72 hours prior to the meeting at novato.org/agendas. 

Watch Meetings

Watch meetings live in-person or streamed online at novato.org/watchmeetings.

City Council meetings are rebroadcast Thursdays at 10:00 AM on Comcast Channel 27 and Planning Commission meetings are rebroadcast Wednesdays at 10:00 AM on Comcast Channel 27. Meetings will replay at other times as well when space is available, the rebroadcast schedule can be found online at cmcm.tv.

Past City Council and Planning Commission videos are available online at novato.org/watchmeetings.

Meet Your Councilmember



Join the Novato City Council and staff at an upcoming community pop-up event for an opportunity to discuss City projects and initiatives, ask questions, share feedback, and help us get to know you better!

Through meaningful conversations, we can continue to shape Novato's future and strengthen our community together. 

Upcoming Events

  • Mayor Pro Tem Kevin Jacobs
    Thursday, May 7 from 1pm to 3pm
    Trailhead, 127 San Marin Drive
  • Councilmember Tim O'Connor
    Tuesday, May 19 from 4pm to 6pm
    Downtown Novato Community Farmers' Market, Sherman Avenue

Past Events

  • Mayor Rachel Farac
    Thursday, March 5 from 11am to 1pm
    Tommy's Salsa, 1553 S Novato Blvd
  • Councilmember Sandeep Karkal
    Thursday, March 19 from 11am to 1pm
    Marin Coffee Roasters, 466 Ignacio Blvd (Pacheco Plaza)

  • Councilmember Pat Eklund
    Thursday, April 30 from 1pm to 3pm
    Lucky Cat Bistro, 471 Entrada Drive

Meet the City Council

Mayor Rachel Farac

Contact:
Email: rfarac@novato.gov
Phone: 415.798.7172

Term Information: Currently serves as Mayor; elected to City Council in November 2022; up for re-election November 2026.

District: 2

Biography: Rachel grew up in San Rafael, where public service was a family-held value. Her father and brother are firefighters; her grandmother was a teacher, one grandfather was a mounted police officer, and the other was a union plumber. The Novato City Council appointed Rachel to the Novato Planning Commission, where she served for two years. The Board of Supervisors appointed Rachel to the Mental Health Board, where she authored a resolution and advocated for converting the Mental Health Board to a Behavioral Health Board. Rachel is passionate about environmental policy and met with members of congress to discuss climate change. Rachel bought her first condo in 2007 in Novato and is currently raising her two children, Ethan and Amelia, with her husband, Colm. 

Education: Master of Public Administration- Indiana Wesleyan, Bachelor in Science-Business Management- California State University- Chico, Certificate in Diversity, Equity & Inclusion in the Workplace, University of South Florida.  

Community Involvement/Affiliations: Marin County Mental Health Board Chair and Member (2018-2023) Planning Commission ( 2020-2022), Soroptimist of Novato (2019- current), Novato Leadership Graduate and mentor( 2019-2021),  Sonoma/Napa Junior League member ( 2018- current), and Marin Environmental Forum  Master Class Graduate (2019).

Professional: Rachel is the Hub West Deputy Lead at Michael Baker/Resilience Action Partners, overseeing the FEMA- Community Engagement and Risk Community contract focused on flood mitigation. Rachel manages the strategy, projects, and resourcing needs of the western United States. Before that role, she started Pristine Builders Group, a residential construction company, working in project management and medical device sales. Her first job out of college was working with the small business community in Novato. 

Mayor Pro Tem Kevin Jacobs

Contact:
Email: kjacobs@novato.gov
Phone: (415) 209-8937

Term information: Currently serves as Mayor Pro Tem; elected November 2024; up for re-election in November 2028.

District: 1

Biography: Kevin was born in New York and moved to the West Coast in 1968 after the passing of his father. He grew up in Daly City and later started his family in Pacifica, where he married his high school sweetheart. After 33 years of marriage, she passed away in 2011. In 2012, Kevin was fortunate to meet Sherri, and they have been together for over 11 years, married for the past six. Kevin retired in Novato, where he and Sherri now call home, planning to make it their Forever home.

Community Involvement / Boards / Committees: Kevin has been actively involved in the Novato community for many years, and he was thrilled to retire here in 2020. Shortly after, he joined the Novato Chamber of Commerce and became a member of the Ambassadors Committee. Kevin had previously been involved with the Chamber during his time as a business owner, an experience he found both rewarding and enriching. He is also a proud graduate of the Leadership Novato program, Class of 2001.

In addition to his work with the Chamber, Kevin was appointed to the Design Review Commission in 2022, a position he also held in 2000. He has served on the Chamber’s Government Affairs Committee and Board of Directors and was an active member of the 4th of July Parade Committee, where he contributed in various roles. Kevin's community service began in 1978 when he was appointed to the Board of Directors for the Tanforan Shopping Center in San Bruno. Earlier in life, he also volunteered as a soccer coach, Scout leader, and was a member of Rotary.

Professional: Kevin enjoyed a successful 41-year career in the construction industry, primarily in the Bay Area, where he worked as a Project Manager and Superintendent on large commercial projects. At one point, he also owned a construction company based in Novato. Kevin began his career as a member of the Carpenters Union, and his company was Union-affiliated.

Councilmember Pat Eklund

Contact:
Email: peklund@novato.gov
Phone: 415.883.9116

Term Information: Served as Mayor in 1996-97, 1999-2000, 2003-2004, 2007-2008, 2012-2013, 2015-2016 and 2020-2021; up for re-election in November 2026

District: 4

Biography: Raised in Terra Linda, Pat is a 49-year Novato resident who lives in Ignacio.

Education: Undergraduate degree from San Francisco State University of California;
MS, Public Administration, Golden Gate University; MS, Environmental Management, University of San Francisco

Community Involvement/Boards and Committees: Currently, Pat serves on the Board for the Cities, Counties and Schools Partnership - a joint effort between the League of California Cities, California State Association of Counties, and California School Boards Association where she served as President twice. Pat also serves on the Environmental Quality Policy Committee for the League of California Cities, Executive Board for the Association of Bay Area Governments (ABAG) where she represents the 11 cities throughout Marin County; she also serves on ABAG’s Regional Planning Committee and Administrative Committee. 

Pat also serves on the Major Crimes Task Force for Marin representing the 10 member cities; Board Member of the Hamilton Restoration Advisory Board; Rotarian; neighborhood leader and active community volunteer for local activities such as Career Days in local schools, Native Sons of the Golden West 4th grade essay contest, and Read Across America at Olive Elementary School. Pat also serves as the alternate to the Transportation Authority of Marin (TAM). Pat is also a member of the Novato Historical Guild, Sierra Club, Marin Conservation League, and an honorary member of the Indian Valley Artists. 

Pat has previously served on the Novato Planning Commission, Board Member for the Novato Community Hospital and the Marin County Flood Control Board, President of the League of California Cities, Novato Representative on TAM, and Board Member of the Sonoma-Marin Area Rail Transit District (SMART), Public Safety and Revenue and Taxation Policy Committees for the League of California Cities; and, President of the Cities, Counties, and Schools Partnership.

Professional: Pat retired after serving over 43 years with the federal government where she served 7 years with the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers and 36 years with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) as an Environmental Manager overseeing environmental-protection programs including the Underground Storage Tanks, Ground Water, Ocean Dumping, Wetlands Protection (Section 404) of the Clean Water Act, San Francisco Bay, and the National Pollution Discharge Elimination System programs.

Councilmember Sandeep Karkal

Contact:
Email:  skarkal@novato.gov
Phone: (415) 271-4964

Term Information: Appointed by the City Council, on December 3, 2025, to fill an unexpected vacancy until the next general municipal election in November 2026.

District: 5

Education: Masters’ (California State University, Sacramento) and Bachelors’ (University of Bombay, India) degrees in Civil/Environmental Engineering, and over 35 years in water resources and environmental protection. 

Continuing postgraduate executive training and certification in leadership and finance, University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill. Certification in Special District Leadership and Management, California Special District and Local Government Institute.

Registered Civil Engineer (P.E.), California and Arizona. High grade utilities operator certifications in both states. Member, American Society of Civil Engineers (M. ASCE).

Community Involvement/Boards and Committees: Sandeep currently serves on the Board of Directors of the National Association of Clean Water Agencies (NACWA), Washington DC; the California Association of Sanitation Agencies (CASA), Sacramento; and as President of the Board of Directors of the California Sanitation Risk Management Authority (CSRMA), San Francisco. Locally, he is also a member of the Rotary Club of Ignacio; a former member of the Board of Directors of the Novato Chamber of Commerce; and a graduate of the Chamber’s Leadership Novato program.

Professional: Sandeep is the Novato Sanitary District's General Manager-Chief Engineer.

Councilmember Tim O'Connor

Contact:
Email:  toconnor@novato.gov
Phone: 415.798.6159

Term Information: Appointed by the City Council to fill an unexpected vacancy on February 14, 2023. Elected for a 4-year term in November 2024; up for re-election in 2028.

District: 3

Education: BA in Business & Marketing from Galway-Mayo Institute of Technology.

Community Involvement/Boards and Committees: Tim served on the City’s Finance Advisory Commission since 2016, and prior to his appointment to Council served as Chair of the commission.  He has dedicated years of volunteer time to the Meadow Park HOA in Hamilton, the Bahia HOA and various service committees throughout Novato.

Professional:  Tim is the Director of Finance and Business Services in the Office of the Chancellor at UC Berkeley. 

Compensation
An ordinance sets Council member salaries (currently $400  per month). Council members also receive medical, dental and life insurance benefits, and may qualify for Public Employee Retirement System (PERS) benefits.

Councilmember compensation
Detailed elected official compensation for calendar year 2020

At their September 29, 2020 meeting, while addressing the City's budget deficit, the Novato City Council directed staff to reduce the bi-weekly councilmember stipend by 5% effective on October 25, 2020.*

* Effective July 3, 2022, the Novato City Council salaries will revert back to their pre-pandemic rates.

Statements of Economic Interests

The City Council Members are elected officers identified in Government Code Section 87200 and file statements of economic interests with the City Clerk's office.

Copies of the statements of economic interests filed by the elected officers may be obtained by visiting the offices of the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) at 428 J Street, Suite 620, Sacramento, California 95814 OR the Novato City Clerk' office at 922 Machin Avenue, Novato, CA 94945.

The statements of economic interests for some state and local government agency elected officers may be available in electronic format on the FPPC's website.
